title: 7 Incoming Clearing Reports
body_text:
7	Incoming Clearing Reports
7.1	Overview of Incoming Clearing Reports
The reports described in this chapter provide information on the credit unionГўВҖВҷs Incoming Clearing Items. These reports are available in the MERG file which can be downloaded from Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange. MERG files are in the credit unionГўВҖВҷs root directory. If applicable, PDFs of the CHQ4 report are available in the Banking folder. PDFs of the CLI, CLO, CLSR, CLUS, SQC, and SUSC reports are available in the Clearing folder. Go to the CAD subfolder to access Canadian dollar reports and the USD subfolder for US dollar reports. PDFs of the PADB and UADB reports are available in the AFT folder. Go to the CAD subfolder to access Canadian dollar reports and the USD subfolder for US dollar reports.
See the File Exchange and Reports Distribution Part for more information on accessing the File and Report Exchange.
7.2	CHQ4 ГўВҖВ“ Unqualified/Returned Cheque Adjustment Debit Notice
Description
ГўВҖВ“ The CHQ4 report describes the adjustments that Central 1 issues for Unqualified Incoming Clearing Items, deposit items, and miscellaneous credit or debit memos. Adjustments are provided in Canadian or US dollars as appropriate.
Note:	If a credit or debit memo is generated by the Ontario office of Central 1, the details are reported on the ADVC report. The volume of these adjustments should be very low after April 1, 2012.
Purpose
ГўВҖВ“ Credit unions use the CHQ4 report to view the adjustments CentralВ 1 creates for Unqualified Incoming Clearing Items, deposit items, and miscellaneous credit and debit memos. This report can also assist credit unions in reconciling their Central 1 accounts.
Organization
ГўВҖВ“ One page is produced per adjustment as follows:
Unqualified/Return Cheque Adjustment Slip ГўВҖВ“ This page describes adjustments that Central 1 creates for Unqualified Incoming Clearing Items and foreign funds Chargebacks. Multiple items may be included in one adjustment. The individual item amounts are provided at the bottom of the page. A copy of the adjustment slip is also uploaded to the File and Report Exchange. See Figure 1.
Credit/Debit Memo Slip ГўВҖВ“ This page describes credit or debit memos that Central 1 creates. The reason for the adjustment is provided at the bottom of the page. See Figure 2.
Distribution
ГўВҖВ“ The CHQ4 report is available to the credit union the day after the items are processed at Central 1.
Figure 1: CHQ4 ГўВҖВ“ Unqualified/Return Cheque Adjustment Slip
Figure 2: CHQ4 ГўВҖВ“ Credit/Debit Memo Slip
7.3	CLI ГўВҖВ“ In-Province Clearings Credit Union Clearing List
Description
ГўВҖВ“ The CLI report lists individual Canadian dollar Incoming Clearing Items that cleared in Vancouver, Toronto, Montreal, or Halifax. See Figure 3. These are considered In-Province Items. The following items are included in the CLI report:
Canadian dollar cheques drawn on the credit union or a member of the credit union
Canadian dollar drafts drawn on the credit union
Canadian dollar money orders drawn on the credit union
Canadian dollar Chargebacks (items dishonoured by another financial institution)
Unqualified Items arenГўВҖВҷt included in this report.
Purpose
ГўВҖВ“ Credit unions use the CLI report for tracing purposes and to assist in reconciling their Central 1 accounts.
Organization
ГўВҖВ“ Reports are produced by branch. Each item appears on one line and includes the account number, amount, and trace number. Items are grouped as follows:
Unpostables ГўВҖВ“ Items for which the account number couldnГўВҖВҷt be read by Central 1ГўВҖВҷs equipment or there is no account number.
Postables ГўВҖВ“ Items that can be read by Central 1ГўВҖВҷs equipment. Items with an ГўВҖВҳ*ГўВҖВҷ are paper business pre-authorized debits.
Distribution
ГўВҖВ“ The CLI report is available to the credit union the day after the items are processed at Central 1.
Figure 3 ГўВҖВ“ CLI Report
7.4	CLO ГўВҖВ“ Out-of-Province Clearings Credit Union Clearing List
Description
ГўВҖВ“ The CLO report lists individual Canadian dollar Incoming Clearing Items that cleared in Calgary or Winnipeg. See Figure 4. These are considered Out-of-Province Items. The following items are included in the CLO report:
Canadian dollar cheques drawn on the credit union or a member of the credit union
Canadian dollar drafts drawn on the credit union
Canadian dollar money orders drawn on the credit union
Canadian dollar Chargebacks (items dishonoured by another financial institution)
Unqualified Items arenГўВҖВҷt included in this report.
Purpose
ГўВҖВ“ Credit unions use the CLO report for tracing purposes and to assist in reconciling their Central 1 accounts.
Organization
ГўВҖВ“ Reports are produced by branch. Each item appears on one line and includes the account number, amount, and trace number. Items are grouped as follows:
Unpostables ГўВҖВ“ Items for which the account number couldnГўВҖВҷt be read by Central 1ГўВҖВҷs equipment or there is no account number.
Postables ГўВҖВ“ Items that can be read by Central 1ГўВҖВҷs equipment. Items with an ГўВҖВҳ*ГўВҖВҷ are paper business pre-authorized debits.
Distribution
ГўВҖВ“ The CLO report is available to the credit union the day after the items are processed at Central 1.
Figure 4 ГўВҖВ“ CLO Report
7.5	CLSR ГўВҖВ“ Credit Union Clearing List Summary
Description
ГўВҖВ“ The CLSR report provides a summary of Incoming Clearing Item totals from the CLI and CLO reports. The number of items and total dollar values are separated by In-Province and Out-of-Province Items. Grand totals are also provided. Unqualified Items arenГўВҖВҷt included in this report. See Figure 5.
Note:	The out-of-region summary will always display zeros.
Purpose
ГўВҖВ“ Credit unions use the CLSR report to determine the amount debited to each branch for Incoming Clearing Items. This report can assist credit unions in reconciling their Central 1 accounts.
Organization
ГўВҖВ“ Reports are produced by branch. The information is organized into four columns: Out-of-Province Summary, Out-of-Region Summary (always zero), In-Province Summary, and total. In each column, the total dollar value and item count is separated by item type.
Distribution
ГўВҖВ“ The CLSR report is available to the credit union the day after the items are processed at Central 1.
Figure 5: CLSR Report
7.6	CLUS ГўВҖВ“ Credit Union Clearing List for US Dollar Items
Description
ГўВҖВ“ The CLUS report lists individual US dollar Incoming Clearing Items for all regions. See Figure 6 and Figure 7. The following items are included in the CLUS report:
US dollar cheques drawn on a US dollar account of the credit union or member of the credit union
US dollar cheques drawn on a Canadian dollar account of a member
US dollar drafts drawn on the credit union
US dollar Chargebacks (items dishonoured by another financial institution)
Unqualified Items arenГўВҖВҷt included in this report.
Purpose
ГўВҖВ“ Credit unions use the CLUS report for tracing purposes and to assist in reconciling their Central 1 accounts. This report is also used to manually debit membersГўВҖВҷ accounts for US dollar items that are drawn on a Canadian dollar account.
Organization
ГўВҖВ“ Reports are produced by branch. Each item appears on one line and includes the account number, amount, and trace number. Items are grouped as follows:
US Account 6404 Clearings ГўВҖВ“ Items that are drawn on a US dollar account.
US Non Account 6404 Clearings ГўВҖВ“ Items that arenГўВҖВҷt drawn on a US dollar account (i.e., the member used a Canadian dollar cheque and wrote ГўВҖВңUS FundsГўВҖВқ). Use this information to debit the memberГўВҖВҷs account.
Distribution
ГўВҖВ“ The CLUS report is available to the credit union the day after the items are processed at Central 1.
Figure 6: CLUS Report ГўВҖВ“ Items Drawn on a US Account
Figure 7: CLUS Report ГўВҖВ“ Items Drawn on a Canadian Dollar Account
7.7	PADB ГўВҖВ“ Information Report ГўВҖВ“ Business Pre-authorized Debits (Canadian Dollar)
Description
ГўВҖВ“ The PADB report provides a list of incoming Canadian dollar Business pre-authorized debits (PADs). Paper and electronic Business PADs are included in the report. See Figure 8 and Figure 9.
Purpose
ГўВҖВ“ Credit unions use the PADB report to identify Business PADs initiated by other financial institutions which are debited to their membersГўВҖВҷ accounts. In certain situations, the recourse period for Business PADs is less than that of other PAD types. If the credit unionГўВҖВҷs internal policy is to notify members of Business PADs, it can use this report to identify the PADs.
Organization
ГўВҖВ“ Reports are produced by branch. The details of each transaction are provided on three lines. Paper Business PADs and electronic Business PADs are reported on separate pages. The page heading for paper items is ГўВҖВңInformation Report ГўВҖВ“ Business Pre-authorized Debits Paper Based.ГўВҖВқ The page heading for electronic Business PADs is ГўВҖВңInformation Report ГўВҖВ“ Business Pre-authorized Debits ГўВҖВ“ AFT.ГўВҖВқ The AFT details are the same as those that appear on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD).
Distribution
ГўВҖВ“ The PADB report is available to the credit union the day after the items are processed at Central 1. The section on paper Business PADs is only produced if there are Business paper PADs for the credit union. The section on electronic Business PADs will be produced even if there are no transactions to report.
Figure 8: PADB Report ГўВҖВ“ Paper Items
Figure 9: PADB Report ГўВҖВ“ AFT Items
7.8	SQC ГўВҖВ“ Summary of CAN Qualified Clearings
Description
ГўВҖВ“ The SQC report provides the Canadian dollar Incoming Clearing Item total by branch. That is, the CLSR report total for each branch is listed on the SQC. See Figure 10.
Unqualified Items arenГўВҖВҷt included in this report.
Purpose
ГўВҖВ“ Credit unions use the SQC report to determine the amount debited to each branch for Qualified Canadian dollar Incoming Clearing Items. This report can help credit unions reconcile their accounts with Central 1.
Organization
ГўВҖВ“ The item count and dollar amount of Qualified Canadian dollar Incoming Clearing Items is provided on the SQC report, one line per branch. The grand total is provided at the bottom of the page, but it is for information only. The grand total isnГўВҖВҷt posted to the credit unionГўВҖВҷs account. Instead, the debits are posted by branch.
Distribution
ГўВҖВ“ The SQC report is available to the credit union the day after the items are processed at Central 1.
Figure 10: SQC Report
7.9	SUSC ГўВҖВ“ Summary of US Qualified Clearings
Description
ГўВҖВ“ The SUSC report provides the US dollar Incoming Clearing Item total by branch. That is, the CLUS report total for each branch is listed on the SUSC. See Figure 11.
Unqualified Items arenГўВҖВҷt included in this report.
Purpose
ГўВҖВ“ Credit unions use the SUSC report to determine the amount debited to each branch for Qualified US dollar Incoming Clearing Items. This report can help credit unions reconcile their accounts with Central 1.
Organization
ГўВҖВ“ The item count and dollar amount of Qualified US dollar Incoming Clearing Items is provided on the SUSC report, one line per branch. The grand total is provided at the bottom of the page, but it is for information only. The grand total isnГўВҖВҷt posted to the credit unionГўВҖВҷs account. Instead, the debits are posted by branch.
Distribution
ГўВҖВ“ The SUSC report is available to the credit union the day after the items are processed at Central 1.
Figure 11: SUSC Report
7.10	UADB ГўВҖВ“ Information Report ГўВҖВ“ Business Pre-authorized Debits (US Dollar)
Description
ГўВҖВ“ The UADB report provides a list of incoming US dollar Business pre-authorized debits (PADs). Paper and electronic Business PADs are included in the report. See Figure 12 and Figure 13.
Purpose
ГўВҖВ“ Credit unions use the UADB report to identify Business PADs initiated by other financial institutions, which are debited to their membersГўВҖВҷ accounts. In certain situations, the recourse period for Business PADs is less than that of other PAD types. If the credit unionГўВҖВҷs internal policy is to notify members of Business PADs, it can use this report to identify the PADs.
Organization
ГўВҖВ“ Reports are produced by branch. The details of each transaction are provided on three lines. Paper Business PADs and electronic Business PADs are reported on separate pages. The page heading for paper items is ГўВҖВңInformation Report ГўВҖВ“ Business Pre-authorized Debits Paper Based.ГўВҖВқ The page heading for electronic Business PADs is ГўВҖВңInformation Report ГўВҖВ“ Business Pre-authorized Debits ГўВҖВ“ AFT.ГўВҖВқ The AFT details are the same as those that appear on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (UCD).
Distribution
ГўВҖВ“ The UADB report is available to the credit union the day after the items are processed at Central 1.
Figure 12: UADB Report ГўВҖВ“ Paper Items
Figure 13: UADB Report ГўВҖВ“ AFT Items
END
